Folder and File Descriptions for Client Mono-Repo,,
Root Directory,,,,,,,,,
• _meta: Contains metadata and configuration files relevant to the entire mono-repo.,,,,,,,,,
Folder Structure,,,,,,,,,
• /src: This folder houses all source code files for the project.,,,,,,,,,
◦ /components: Reusable UI components.,,,,,,,,,
◦ /services: Business logic and API service integration.,,,,,,,,,
◦ /utils: Utility functions and helpers used across the application.,,,,,,,,,
• /tests: Includes all test files organized by the corresponding source files.,,,,,,,,,
◦ /unit: Unit tests for individual components and functions.,,,,,,,,,
◦ /integration: Integration tests for testing the interaction between components.,,,,,,,,,
• /docs: Documentation for the project,,,,,,including setup instructions and API references.,,,
• /scripts: Contains scripts for building,,,,,,testing,and deploying the application.,,
File Descriptions,,,,,,,,,
• http://readme.md/: Overview of the project,,,,,,setup instructions,and contribution guidelines.,,
• package.json: Lists project dependencies,,,,,,scripts,and metadata for the project.,,
• .gitignore: Specifies files and directories that should be ignored by Git version control.,,,,,,,,,
• tsconfig.json: TypeScript configuration file if the project is using TypeScript.,,,,,,,,,

Description:
Folder and File Descriptions for Client Mono-Repo,,
Root Folder: apps,,,,,,,,,
This is the main directory that contains all the application-related folders and files for the client mono-repo.,,,,,,,,,
Subfolders:,,,,,,,,,
1. app1:,,,,,,,,,
◦ Description: Contains the source code,,,,,,assets,and configuration files for the first application.,,
◦ Key Files:,,,,,,,,,
▪ index.js: Main entry point for the application.,,,,,,,,,
▪ config.json: Configuration settings specific to app1.,,,,,,,,,
2. app2:,,,,,,,,,
◦ Description: Holds the complete codebase for the second application,,,,,,including any necessary libraries and dependencies.,,,
▪ main.js: Main script that initializes the application.,,,,,,,,,
▪ settings.xml: XML file for application settings.,,,,,,,,,
3. shared:,,,,,,,,,
◦ Description: A shared directory that contains common utilities,,,,,,components,and services used across multiple applications.,,
▪ utils.js: JavaScript utilities for various functions.,,,,,,,,,
▪ styles.css: Common styles used by all applications.,,,,,,,,,
Configuration Files:,,,,,,,,,
• package.json: Lists dependencies,,,,,,scripts,and configurations for the mono-repo.,,
• .gitignore: Specifies files and directories to be ignored by Git.,,,,,,,,,
• README.md: Documentation for understanding the structure and usage of the mono-repo.,,,,,,,,,
Notes:,,,,,,,,,
• Each application folder should follow the same structure to maintain consistency and ease of navigation.,,,,,,,,,
• Shared resources should be updated in the shared directory to reflect changes across applications.

Folder and File Descriptions for Client Mono-Repo,,
Assets Folder,,,,,,,,,
The assets folder contains all the static resources required for the project. This includes images,,,,,,fonts,and any other media files that are used throughout the application.,,
Subfolders,,,,,,,,,
• Images: Contains all image files,,,backgrounds).,,,categorized by type (e.g.,icons,,
• Fonts: Houses custom font files used in the application to ensure consistent typography.,,,,,,,,,
• Videos: Contains any video assets required for promotional or educational content.,,,,,,,,,
Key Files,,,,,,,,,
• http://readme.md/: A markdown file providing an overview of the assets,,,,,,including guidelines on how to use them within the project.,,,
• LICENSE: Contains licensing information for any third-party assets included in the repo.

data/,,,,,,,Folder and File Descriptions for Client Mono-Repo

Directory Name: • data/: Contains all data-related files and directories used across the project.
• raw/: This folder includes the original
Date Modified: unprocessed data files. These files should not be modified directly and are used for reference.

Directory Name: • processed/: Contains cleaned and transformed data files that are ready for analysis or modeling. These files are derived from the raw data and should be well-documented.
• scripts/: This directory holds scripts for data processing
Parent Folder: and any other preprocessing steps required for analysis.

Directory Name: • notebooks/: Contains Jupyter notebooks or similar files where exploratory data analysis (EDA) and visualizations are conducted. These notebooks help in understanding the data better and testing hypotheses.
• http://readme.md/: A file that provides an overview of the data structure
Description: and instructions on how to run scripts or notebooks.

Directory Name: Ensure that every file and folder is properly documented to facilitate easy navigation and understanding for any new team members or stakeholders involved in the project."
